At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Container Hardening model · Theory Container Har…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 7703 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove dependency and browser provenance, package contents, least-authority containers, and immutable rollout; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use pinned multi-stage images, non-root user, read-only runtime where possible, explicit browser dependencies, limits, signals, and inspected contents.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Container Hardening model · Theory Container Har…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Containers sandbox untrusted pages and plugins by default.
